questions.question: Define a Context-Free Grammar. List and briefly explain its four components.
expectedPoints: Formal definition as a 4-tuple: (V, T, P, S)
P: Set of Production rules (A -> β)

questions.question: Consider the grammar G: S -> 0 S 1 | 0 1. Perform a leftmost derivation for the string '000111'. Draw the corresponding parse tree.
expectedPoints: Parse Tree: Root S, with children 0, S, 1. The S child itself has children 0, S, 1, and the innermost S has children 0, 1.
Explanation of how the tree matches the derived string.

questions.question: Explain the problem of ambiguity in context-free grammars. Use the 'dangling else' problem as an example and show two possible parse trees for the string 'if E1 if E2 S1 else S2'.
expectedPoints: Definition of ambiguity: Multiple parse trees for one string.
Description of the 'dangling else' grammar: S -> if (E) S | if (E) S else S | ...
Tree 1: 'else' matches the inner 'if' (if E1 { if E2 S1 } else S2)
Tree 2: 'else' matches the outer 'if' (if E1 { if E2 S1 else S2 })
Discussion on why this is a problem for compiler design.
